page: Microsoft Azure E16d_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDv4 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by 128.0 GB of RAM, which provides an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. This instance includes 614 GB of local SSD storage, but does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results place the E16d_v4 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1287.0 and a multi-core score of 7924.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 15901.98. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block bandwidth of 831.21 GB/sec and a large block write bandwidth of 44.34 GB/sec. For database-related operations, the instance processed 13.96 million Redis SET operations per second and achieved a Passmark database score of 5735.49.

While the instance demonstrates solid capabilities for memory-intensive applications, it exhibits performance tradeoffs in complex computational tasks. For example, LLM inference benchmarks show weak performance for medium 7B models, with prompt processing at 22.4 tokens/sec and text generation at 8.48 tokens/sec, and large 70B models are unsupported. However, the combination of 128 GB of RAM and 614 GB of local storage makes this instance highly cost-efficient for relational databases, in-memory caching, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring high memory density without the need for specialized GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E16d_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv4 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor clocked at 2.6 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local SSD storage. Designed for memory-intensive workloads, it offers an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a Passmark CPU score of 15901.98 and a Redis SET throughput of 13.96 million operations per second. However, it shows weak performance in medium-sized LLM inference tasks and lacks GPU acceleration. The E16d_v4 is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics workloads that benefit from high memory density and local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16d_v4 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, delivering balanced performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16d_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density and local SSD storage for databases and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB RAM
Bundled 614 GB of local SSD storage
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU
Average performance in database operations and Redis caching
Weak performance for LLM inference and lacks GPU acceleration
Ideal for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
